Hey everyone, this is Russell Brunson, welcome back to session number five of the Funalology Masterclass, the new art and science of funnel building. I hope you guys have enjoyed all the sessions so far, we've been covering a lot of stuff, and hopefully giving you guys an understanding of all of the funnel frameworks and how they work, because again, they're not super complicated, they're simple when you start to understand them. So session number one, we talked about the secret formula, figuring out who's your dream customer, where are they congregating, what's the bait you're gonna use to grab them, get their attention, and then where are you gonna take them? And inside there we went deep into hook story offer, which is the framework we use to grab somebody, so we get their attention, tell them a story to build a relationship with them, and increase the perceived value of the offer we're about to make them, and then we make them an offer. And notice I said an offer, and we don't sell them a product, right? We create an offer, because an offer increases the perceived value of the thing we're trying to sell, and it makes us unique, so we're not a commodity, so it's something different that only we can offer. That was all session number one. So if you missed it, make sure you go back and watch that, because it was insane. Session number two, then we talked about the result, like where are we taking somebody? Every single customer who comes into your world, if you have a transactional business, you're gonna always struggle, you're always gonna be working, like looking for the next sale, the next quick way to make cash, right? We don't want transactional business, we want businesses that are transformational, we're transforming people's lives. And so the big question is, when somebody comes into your world, where are you taking them? What's your value ladder look like? What's the AUS, or the big result that your people are looking for? How do you craft that and create it? How do you create a value ladder that grabs somebody and sends them up and provides value so they want more from you, and provides more value so they want more from you, so you can change their lives, so you can get them to the end result they're looking for, and you can help them find your AUS. Which I guess that means that you're the wizard of AUS, which is kind of exciting, so congratulations. That was session number two. Then from there, we started diving into the value ladder, and what happens to each funnel inside the value ladder. The next session was all about lead funnels, right? We talked about the three-in-type of lead funnels that we used to generate leads, and we talked about the reason why you want leads, and why you're trying to build a list, and the value of that list, and why it's so important. Okay, we showed you those funnels. Then we moved up one tier of the value ladder, and we showed you the unboxing funnels. Okay, from there, we showed you the book funnels for any of our information-based businesses, and cart funnels for any of our physical product businesses, and they also went deep into challenge funnels, for anyone who wants to be able to take their customers and doctorate them before they send them up to value ladder. And so we've had a lot of fun so far, and now we're moving up to the next tier of the value ladder, which are our presentation funnels, and that's what session five is all about. So we move up the value ladder, you can see we've got lead funnels, unboxing funnels, you guys are now masters of those levels, of those funnels, those frameworks, and now we're gonna be jumping into presentation funnels. So that's kind of the game plan. Now I showed you guys this during yesterday's presentation, that basically all of these two tiers of the value ladder come off of you making an offer, right? We talked about all during session number one. We created this amazing offer, and we got two ways to sell that offer. Number one is we unboxed that offer, like I showed you guys during a yesterday's session, we said, okay, this first offer's gonna be the first thing, and the second thing, and upsell, and one more, upsell number two, the order form bump, like we unboxed that offer, and we laid out a logical sequencing of offers, okay? Or number two is take the entire offer we created, and we just sell it all at once, okay? This is where we start selling more expensive things, and so the way we sell something for 500 or 1,000, or 2,000, or 5,000 dollars, is by making a presentation, and that presentation increases the perceived value that you're selling, and then you actually sell the person the thing, okay? Now I love all these types of funnels, but presentation funnels are one of my favorite. During the very first introduction session, I talked about Hook's story offer and story selling, how we grab people and we tell stories, like all those things we kind of talked about there are gonna be elements inside of the presentations you are making. Now one thing I would highly recommend, I'm not gonna go deep into it during this training, just because it's outside of the context of what we're covering, but if you've read the expert secrets book, or gone through my perfect webinar training, that presentation is perfect, that's why we call it the perfect webinar, it's the perfect presentation, right? And I would highly recommend studying that, because it doesn't matter if you're using a video sales letter, a webinar, funnel, or a product launch funnel, the script is the same, that's the big secret, okay? It's the same presentation, I use the perfect webinar on my video sales, I use the perfect webinar on my webinars, I use the perfect webinar on my product launch funnel, so that script, that presentation is important to master and to understand because it is where you're gonna be plugging into all these different funnels, okay? And I'll kind of show that a little bit as we go through here, but just for those of you who wanna go deeper, again this whole training of funnelology is more about the funnel structure and funnel frameworks, and then the art is you weaving your stories into it, and so that's the next piece for you to start learning and mastering is the art and how to do those presentations and things like that, okay? So that's what's happening, today though, is we're focusing on presentation funnels. Okay, so the three presentation funnels that we're talking about, the very first one is our video sales letter funnels, and this is, honestly for probably five or six years, this is the only type of funnel that I ever did. A lot of these other variations are things that we've kind of developed and pioneered, a lot more so over the last five or six years, but the first decade we pretty much were doing video sales letter funnels and sales letter funnels, and so I wanna take a step back in time so you understand where these things came from, because sometimes we're like, oh, this is a funnel, like it just came, because some guy was really, really smart and figured it out, and the answer is no, it came from people who are now dead, who are way smarter than us, and we're just basically copying them, okay? If you rewind pre, Facebook pre, MySpace pre, Google pre, internet, okay, back when the way we got all of our communication was either through a phone line or by the postman coming to our house and dropping off letters, this is where this whole concept of sales letters and video sales letters was pioneered, okay? In fact, probably all the way back, actually it's not true, I did some research for the Traffic Secrets book trying to figure out when the first sales letters were ever done, and I don't remember the exact date, but I do remember finding the very first sales letter was basically people were trying to raise money, so they would send these letters in the mail to try to raise money, and so anyway, so if you look at this image here, the old school sales letters, the way that these would work is that a marketer like me or you would go and they'd rent a list and say, okay, I'm selling a fitness product, I'm gonna go find a list of people who've bought in fitness products in the past, and they'd rent the buyer list, and they'd write a letter, a big old sales letter, it had a headline and then a copy, and then a sub-headline, more copy, and then the back would have an order form, they'd take the letter, fold it up, put it in the envelope, and mail it to people who they knew were probably likely to buy some type of weight loss product. They send that letter out, and the city sent out a thousand letters to try to get a 2% or 3% response rate, so they send 100 out, they get three orders, they get 1,000, they get 30 orders, and that was how the whole game was played. Prior to the end, I asked how direct response marketers made their money, either they were doing direct response on radio or on TV or the majority of it was happening through direct mail, and so I don't know about you guys, if you remember getting sales letters like this, you got excited if you just threw them away, but I geeked out on them. My whole journey started back when I was about 11 or 12 years old, you read the original.com secrets book, you remember the story in there, but my dad one night, every night he'd watch the news, and after the news was over, he'd be working doing his business on the side, and he usually made me go to bed, but it was a weekend, and for some reason, he didn't make me go to bed, I was watching the news with him, and the news ended, and I was waiting for him to tell me to go to bed, and he didn't, and I'm like, oh my gosh, this is so cool, and then a few minutes later, Mash came on. You guys remember Mash? It was the next show after the news, every single night, so the Mash came on, I was like, this is amazing, my parents watched Mash, I've never seen it before, and I remember I didn't really get many of the jokes, but it was just cool to be hanging out with my dad, and then Mash got over, all of a sudden, flipped over to these infomercials, and the first infomercial was by this guy named Don the Pre, and he was teaching how to make money with tiny little classified ads, and do you guys remember those ads? If you're not, you can go to YouTube, and Google, go to YouTube, and YouTube's typed Don the Pre, tiny classified ads, you'll see the old infomercials, but these infomercials changed my life, I was sitting there watching, I was like, oh my gosh, and I still remember the story he told, he said, you know, he said, I placed a classified ad in a newspaper, and I made $30 profit, I told my friends and my family, they kind of tease me, and I took that same ad, and I placed it in a thousand newspapers, and I made $30,000 profit. I remember he said that, I was like, what? What freaking out, I'm looking at my dad, he's like doing his taxes, whatever he's doing, I'm like, dad, are you paying attention? Like, he's not, I'm like freaking out, and by the time the infomercial was over, I'm like, I am sold, but as a 12 year old kid, I didn't have the 40 bucks I needed, or a credit card to order this thing, so I'm freaking out, and I wrote down the phone number, I told my dad, I'm like, dad, we need to get this, and my dad kind of smiled, like, you know, okay, I'm like, no, we have to have this, and he finally told me, he's like, well, I'm not gonna buy it for you, but if you wanna work for it, you can, so you may go out and mow the lawns, and do gardening, and for the next six weeks, whatever, I did everything I could to earn my 40 bucks, and I finally got done. I remember, this is kind of a funny side story, I remember I had the phone number I was gonna call, but I was like, I remember in the infomercial, I said that if you call in the next five minutes, you get these three other bonuses, three other bonuses, and I was like, dad, I have to have those bonuses, so we can't call right now, we need to get the infomercial again, and I called during the infomercial, so I don't miss out on these three bonuses, and I remember, like, sitting there every night with him, I tell him, like, if it starts playing the ad, come wake me up, come wake me up, and so a couple nights, one night, comes in, he rusted the plane, the ad, I'm like, what, I run out there, I get all excited, and we watch, as soon as the phone number comes out, I call the number on the phone, and then I hand it to my dad, and he took his credit card in, and a week later, I get this kit in the mail, and I'm, like, freaking out, reading all this stuff, and it was awesome. The biggest problem I had is that as a 12-year-old kid, I didn't have the money to place a classified ad or a product to sell, I didn't know what to do, but I was obsessed, I got the gist. I remember, like, a couple weeks later, I was with my mom at the grocery store, walking around, and we saw this magazine, it was the Small Business Opportunity magazine, which, if you buy a magazine, you should always see it there, it was always, like, cartoon faces on, like, how to get rich quick through whatever, and I looked at it, I was like, oh my gosh, I wanna get rich quick, so I got it, I'm flipping through, and there's all these ads in there, I'm like, mom, can you please buy this for me? She's like, you're ridiculous, and finally, she said, yes, pays the five bucks, buys the magazine for me, I get home, and I start opening it up, and every single page is an ad for a new way to make money, like, one was like, how to sell gold chains at the mall to make money, one was how to paint glow-in-the-dark stars on the ceiling to make money for people, like, all these ideas, and every single one of them had a 1-800 number to go to order a free information kit, so I started calling every single one, like, literally, I think it was 144-page magazine, and there were four pages of articles and 140 pages of ads, so I called all 140 of them, and what happened is that every single person sent me out their info kit, and then guess what? They sold my name to other director-sponsored marketers, and they sold to other people, and soon I was getting hundreds of letters every single day in the mail, and it was amazing, so I tell you the story because I was like a 12-year-old kid, and I would get these sales letters in the mail, and I get it, and I look at the envelope, and the headline, on the envelope, they'd have, sometimes they have a copy on the envelope, like, you open up, learn how to make money at your local mall, I'm like, oh, and I open it, I pull it out, it's this huge letter, I'd read page one, and page two, and I read every single one of them, and then they'd always have an order form, and I'm like, ah, and I remember as I was doing that, there was two buckets, one I read, I'm like, that sounds stupid, one I'd be like, this is the greatest idea of all time, I must make my parents buy a credit card to order this kit, and have the two different piles, and so for me, as a 12-year-old dorky kid, I was reading sales letters, I was just obsessed with this thing, right? And a few years after I got into wrestling, and I forgot about selling stuff, and I got obsessed with wrestling, and then I wrestled at BYU, they cut the wrestling program, I went on a mission for two years for my church, after that I went to Boise State, and I started wrestling in Boise State, and about that time is when I met my beautiful wife, I proposed to her, she said yes, without knowing that I had zero dollars, and no job and no prospects, because I was wrestling, and we decided to get married about two or three weeks before we got married, I was like, I gotta figure out how to pay for stuff, because I have zero dollars. It's funny how these things don't go through your mind as you're proposing and everything, it's just like later, you're like, huh, how are we gonna eat, anyway. And so I'm sitting there, and that night I see an ad for this how to get rich quick on the internet thing, and it was like this event that's happening at local Holiday Inn, which is actually not too far from here, and I was like, oh my gosh, I'm gonna get rich on the internet, so I called the number, got the tickets, went to Holiday Inn, and I'm sitting there, and I remember the dude sitting there talking, and he said, he starts showing these things, he said, this is a sales letter, it's like a big long letter, and it's a webpage, has this huge letter, and when we go and buy ads, we drive people to sales letter, and if one percent of people read it, they buy the product, you make money, and as I'm looking at it, I was like, oh my gosh, this is exactly what everyone was doing with sales letters, like back when I was a teenager, right? They were finding a list of people who were interested in the topic, they'd write along the form sales letter, they put it in an envelope, put a stamp on it, mail it to somebody, and hopefully one or two percent of people bought the thing, and that's when they made money. This same thing, instead of having a print thing, it's just this long website, the sales letter, you drive people who are interested to the sales letter, and try to get one or two percent of people to buy, and my mind was just blown, I was like, I already know what they're doing, this is amazing, I started freaking out, and that was where I got started in this business, okay? And from there, I started finding all these websites that long form sales letters, and at first, I was just always confused by it, because I was like, why has someone read this so long, but then the right sales letters, the ones that had information that I was interested in, I would read 60, 70 pages a copy sometimes, and by the time I got to the bottom, I was like, I must give these people money, like I have to, right? I'm so mostly, I've been sucked into a really good sales letter, and so for the next, man, probably seven or eight years of my business, that was what it was, we were driving long form sales letters, we'd find a product, create an offer, make a long form sales letter, drive traffic, that thing, and that was how we did everything. And this is about the time that YouTube started coming out, and people started doing videos, but the problem we do videos in the band was so hard, it was so expensive, and videos were super choppy, and if you had too many videos on a page, the whole page would crash, and it just wasn't, it just wasn't like efficient to do videos, so some people were like, oh yeah, this is the future, we're gonna make video infomercials, and it never really got to that, but it just, it took a long time, and I remember a couple years later, I had this friend, his name was Keith Wellman, and Keith one day had an idea, and he took his long form sales letter, and he turned it into a video, but it was crazy, all it was was like a video, you've probably seen some of these before, it was like the first sentence, and then he read it, it was like, hey guys, welcome to my website, and that showed the second sentence, over the next three minutes, I'm gonna teach you boba, and then the next sentence, like it basically read the sales letter to you, and I remember watching it, I was like, this is so weird, and it's frustrating, I wanna scroll and scan through and see everything, but I couldn't, because there's no way to scroll or scan, it was just this video, and it was like sentence after sentence after sentence, and I got so hooked in, I wanna sit with the next sentence, and I sat there and I watched this whole thing for like 20, 25 minutes, and when I was done, then he had an order of the link pop open, and to go sell the product, and I remember watching it, I was like, that was fascinating, I couldn't stop watching this thing, and then I messaged Keith, I was like, how's it doing? And he told me, I can't remember the sales, but it was over a million dollars during the Christmas vacations, like I had no product launched or nothing, I had to send an email out to my list for this thing, and people went crazy for it, and from then, after that people started getting obsessed with video sales letters, and video sales letters, at first, were just basically a long form sales letter, but instead of like someone reading it, just like, here's the headline, here's sentence number one, here's sentence number two, and like, the person would read it as it was appearing one sentence at a time, okay? And they still actually work really effectively, you probably see a lot of people doing them, I haven't done one for a while, but they still work really well, and so for the next, man, probably five or six years, everyone's shifted to video sales letters, and it was just video sales letters, and no copy whatsoever, it's just video, and that was it. And I think that it was so unique at the time, like people said, they'd start a video, and they're like, I can't believe they watched the whole video, in fact, it's five or six years, like those work the best in the world. Now fast forward to today, I've found, and I'm not sure if it's true in every market, but most markets I've dabbled in and played with, it's kind of a blend of both, it's not just a sales letter or just a video, it's a blend of both, where there's a video at the top, right, and then the sales letters down below, and a lot of times it's the exact same thing, word for word, but you have different modalities that people like to learn from, right, and like to listen. So I'm curious for you guys to listen to right now, how many of us right now like to consume things through video like this, like you're like, I just want to watch a video, have someone explain it to me. Okay, if you're a video person, you're number one. Okay, number two, if you're something like, I don't like video, I just don't want to read something, I want to scroll through and be able to read and get the ballpoint really fast, you're my readers, who are my readers? Okay, so if you're a video person, type in video down below, type video, okay. Now if you're a text person, write text down below, I just want to see like text versus audio, how many there are, okay, text versus video. My guess is it's probably like 50, 50, half of you guys don't want to watch videos, half of you guys don't want to read text, and my guess is for most part, if it's video, you're just going to bounce, or if it's a text, you're going to be like, you're going to go with the modality like the most. And so I found for highest conversion today, and this may shift tomorrow, who knows, it's having a video up top for the people who want video and having the long form cells down below, but it's typically the same copy, but one's for my view, my watchers, one's for my readers. And I noticed like if you watch the analytics on a page, you'll see your viewers sitting there to watch the whole video, whereas your readers come, they watch like three seconds, they pause, they scroll down, they start reading, okay? And so I like having both. And so most of my videos, salesletters have both elements in it, okay? All right, so there's a very long story to get you to where we are today for video sales letter funnel, okay? So here's what a video sales letter funnel looks like. Again, typically I'm going to be selling, that they're kind of similar to the unboxing funnels, but you notice that the order form's not at the top, because I'm selling something more expensive, so I need more time to explain what it is before I ask somebody for money. Okay, with no brainer like low ticket, free plus shipping of 10, 20, 30 bucks, a lot of times it's okay to have order form up high because it doesn't take a lot for me to convince somebody to buy. If I'm selling a $500 product, I want to make sure I have the presentation pretty close to done before I say, here's what the price is, okay? And so you notice the order form is usually down below or it's on a separate page, okay? And so this is kind of the structure, there's a video down below that's going to be called a video spoiler box, there's an add to cart button and down below that I'll have like typically my long form sales letter down below. We have order form bump and then upsell one, two, and thank you. Now I can go deep into upsell one, two, and thank you because those are very similar to what we did in the unboxing funnel. So you haven't watched those videos yet, go back, watch the unboxing videos because it's the same concept. If I'm selling information product, my upsells are going to be the next logical thing. If I'm selling physical products, it's going to be more of the same thing at a discount. So that's what you just got to kind of understand. The upsell pages are very almost identical to the unboxing funnels. It's just the video sales letter is the thing that's different in this funnel. So here's an example of video sales letter we did. This is for the old funnel university. We had a head on top and there's a video of me and this one I didn't do PowerPoint style. It was me basically doing my presentation just like I would do a webinar. But this became my webinar and the entire presentation was here. Okay, so that's how the video sales letter worked. And so because I'm thinking, well, Russell, what do I say inside my video sales letter? So I want to do two things. Number one, I want to go back to presentation number one to kind of touch upon it. The very first session we talked about hook story offer. But number two, if you want to go deep and really understand this, you can study my trainings on the perfect webinar and help you understand exactly how to craft this. But basically comes back to what I say in the video, you are telling your Pifny Bridge story. Someone's coming to this video, they're just like you, you have to remember this. You have to remember what was the emotional experience you had, what's the Pifny Bridge you had, they got you over here. You got to remember no technical babble, you come back and you're telling them your story. That's the core concept of what's happening there. And this is the perfect webinar framework. I told you guys on session one, there's four core stories, right? The first shot at the big domino is your origin story. So if you've come to my videos like, hey, I'm Russell Brunson, let me tell you my story about why I think funnels are the fastest way for you to grow your company. And I told the origin story about my Pifny Bridge origin story about why I believe that the thing I'm about to offer is the greatest thing in the world. Okay, right? And from there I come down to say, okay, what's the vehicle? What are their false beliefs about the vehicle? Now I'm gonna tell them the story, then hopefully gonna trump their story that breaks the false belief they had about the vehicle. Then I move to the second part of this, or the secret number two, which is their internal beliefs. Like what's the thing, per se? I believe this is a good thing, but I don't think I can do it. Then I tell a story about that, boom. And then third thing, again, I believe funnels are the greatest thing in the world. I believe I can actually do it, but I can do it because I'm not gonna drive traffic, right? Or I believe that Keto's the best diet for me. I believe I can actually do it, but my wife's gonna buy ice cream, I know, and then I'm gonna fall out of ketosis, and the whole thing's gonna fall apart. The external belief, right? So I tell the third story about the external belief, and from there I move down, I do the stack and the close. Okay? And again, if you've read the expert's secrets book, I don't have to do any of my perfect webinar training. This is stuff we talk about all the time, so I'm not gonna go deep into it, but it's basically, it's me telling my four core stories, and then making my offer through the stack and the close. Okay? That's what I put in here. Okay, so I put into this video right here. Okay? All right. And then if my story's trumped theirs, that's the sales video. That's the sales that if my story's trumped theirs, it knocks down the big domino, and they become a customer. That's it. Okay? So that's the first part of the page. I have it on the top. I have the video sales letter. And then below the video, I have what I call a video spoiler box. I tried to give it to him to call it the Brunson box, but nobody ever does, so if you wanna like, kiss up to me, call it the Brunson box, if you wanna call it what it is, it's a video spoiler box. So the video spoiler box, the goal of the video spoiler box is to get someone who's coming down to see the video, like, oh my God, watch this not, and they start kind of going down, and you have this opportunity to grab them before they leave. It's almost like a preview or a trailer in a movie, right? Like, you see the trailer, you're like, okay, I wanna watch that. That's what the video spoiler box is, is to kind of grab really quick, and be like, wait, don't bounce on the video, this is where you're gonna learn a video, and hopefully hook them a couple times, so like, oh yeah, I wanna be a part of this video, I wanna watch this, right? So for me, usually, the video spoiler box, I've got four different things here. Number one is the hook, it's a hook, that's inside the video that I'm gonna hook them with, right? So like, in this one, for example, a secret number one, tight-nose weeks can make huge changes. I'm gonna show you four, four of my top split tests that are so simple, and you can use it up in just minutes, boom, there's a hook, right? And a story and an offer, like all these things are in there, like watch this video, because you're learning this right here. Number two, here's the next hook, boom, not all people buy the same way, here's the story, let me show you the secret step that had before my funnels allowed me to know exactly how to get them to buy, boom, and then the offers, like, go watch the video, okay? Number three, see, there are only three funnels that actually matter, we found that 90% of our income came from just three funnels, if you're focusing on anything else, you're just flushing your money down the toilet, hook, story, and the video, the offers go watch the video, right? And then number four here is, what's the next step? Get your 14-day trial membership to funnel, so this is like where we're going, hook, story, and then go watch the video for the offer, okay? And so that's the video spoiler box, that gets them to actually go back and like, ah, I gotta figure out what is number three, what's number two, like it gets them to go back and consume the actual video, okay? It's crazy to me, like all the highest converting offers I see online, from the big, huge brands, the big companies, all have video spoiler box, and I almost see nobody in our industry ever use these, which blows my mind, but they work very, very, very, very, very good, okay? So here's the spoiler box, step in one here, inside of the funnel. Is that right there? And again, if you're doing a sales letter version underneath the outer carbon, then I have the whole long form version of the sales letter down there, and then from there, the rest of the funnel is the same, same as unboxing funnels, okay? We have an order form bump, we have episode one, episode two, and thank you page, where we can send them to the next funnel, or whatever happens in that step, okay? So that is the video sales letter funnel, that's the first type of presentation funnel. All right, now we move on to funnel number two, which is a webinar funnel. The most you guys know that the way we grew click funnels, we use a lot of them type of funnels, but the primary way, especially initially, to get us from zero to 10,000 customers, was 100% through webinar funnels. We just had one funnel, and we perfected it, and we did over and over and over and over and over again. If you read the expert secrets book, the original version, I go through D, but how we did it, and how it worked, and the process, and basically, we did a webinar at least once a week, every single week, we spent the whole week filling the webinar, we did the webinar, we closed it out, and we kept doing that process over and over and over again, okay? I still think if I was to start a new company from scratch, the very first funnel I would focus on is webinar funnel. That'd be the very first thing I would do immediately to get started. I think the majority of people come to my world today, I can wrestle, I learn about all the funnels, you know, which one should I do first? Most of you guys, this is the one I'd recommend you do first. Not everybody, like if you're selling physical products, I probably would start with the webinar, but if you're selling any type of information products, any type of coaching, consulting, all those kind of things, webinar funnel is still my favorite, so take that for what it's worth. All right, webinar funnels. This is what a basic webinar funnel looks like, it's very simple and very basic. There's a page people register, there's a thank you page, thank you for registering, then they actually have the webinar that happens somewhere, and then at the end of the webinar, you sell them your offer, okay? There's an order form, that's kind of it. It's very simple and very easy, right? So for example, this is the webinar funnel, the built click funnels, okay? It's not difficult, it's not complicated, so there's some strategy to understand it, again, there's the framework, and then the things we weave into the framework, so let me walk you through that. So you saw the framework, let me walk you through the art that goes into it, okay? So the first is the registration page. Now a couple things on the registration page, the more curiosity you weave into the registration page, the higher your registration rate will be, okay? Listen to how much curiosity is in this headline. My weird niche funnel is currently making me $17,947 per day, and how to ethically knock it off in less than 10 minutes. Okay, my weird niche funnel, that causes curiosity, is currently making me $17,000 a day? Like what? $17,000 a day, and how to ethically knock it off, how do you ethically knock something off? It makes no logical sense whatsoever. You're doing less than 10 minutes, like there's so much curiosity, even the image, why is Russell like, hey, loose, what's he doing, he's holding a webpage, and it's just weird, right? There's so much curiosity, you're like, I don't know what this is, but I need to register, okay? So this is what we used to launch click funnels, and I was doing this webinar, honestly it was three to five times a week during the first four or five months of click funnels, and we got down to like two times a week, and then by the end of the week, once a week, and this whole guy from zero to first 10,000 members, was by me doing this webinar over and over, and over and over again, okay? That was the hook that got people in. Okay, so that's page number one. Now if they'd registered, then the next page is our thank you page, they'd say thank you so much for registering, the webinar's happening Tuesday, it's happening Friday, whatever that thing might be, okay? Here I like to give them reminders, like don't forget, put on your calendar, like all the things they're not gonna forget about the webinar, okay? And then the next thing you can do here, is you can, there's a couple of different methodologies, but basically it's like you can make them an offer on thank you page, if the webinar's not for two more days, like you have this gap right now, what if you create something that you put on thank you page that you could then sell to recoup the advertising costs that you spent to get someone to buy here? Okay, sometimes you call us a self-liquidating offer, so I got put an offer here like, hey before you come to the webinar, go buy this $20 e-book that's gonna help you understand blah, it's gonna help you when you get on the webinar, right, something like that. So this is a great spot where you can sell something, well we did, we launched click funnels, we told people go get a two free trial, then register for webinars, so the webinar's coming up, to prepare for the webinar, go get a free trial of the click funnels, and just try it out. And so we got people to go and create an account here, and create their account click funnels before they came, and I also said, get account click funnels and buy your dot com stickers book. So by getting everyone to register for webinar, we should have pushed them to the next steps in our value ladder, go get a free trial, go buy my book, and get sent into the buying process, okay? So that's kind of what happens, registration page, thank you page. Now inside click funnels, we don't have software that actually runs the webinar, okay, so the ones that we typically use either go to webinar or zoom, are two amazing webinar platforms, and they integrate into click funnels, okay? So if you look at page number one, this page right here, this is built in click funnels, and I integrated either go to webinar or zoom into this funnel, so when someone clicks on it, they put the email address in, it goes and creates the webinar inside a zoom or go to webinar, okay? So then what happens now is then, when the webinar happens Thursday at two, or whenever you're doing it, boom, they log in to go to webinar, log into zoom, and there's a live video of you doing your presentation, okay? And that's it. So this is an example of a go to webinar that me and Jim Edwards did, where we were selling funnel scripts, okay? It's just a live presentation of us selling our thing. Now I'm wondering, well Russell, what do I say to this presentation? Well, let me guess, you should guess. You're gonna do this, you're gonna tell your epiphyte of your story about why you came out of this thing, why it's so exciting, okay? You're gonna tell your origin story, at the very beginning, you're first pushed to the big domino, they're gonna tell you the story about your vehicle, about internal beliefs, your external beliefs, and you're gonna do the stack in the clothes, okay? Literally perfect webinar 101, you're gonna go watch that training, you're gonna read the extra stickers book, master it, and that's the presentation you give, okay? And if you do that, you do the right, all dominoes will follow, on the end of the webinar, they're gonna rush to the order form to give you money. And one big thing to understand, we tested this like crazy, on the order form, you don't want to go and do along form cells that are re-explaining everything. I've seen more people talk someone out of a cell that are order form than anything else. But if they go to your order form, they are ready to buy. All you do is you just let them buy, that's it. Don't, I used to have a recap, like here's everything you're getting in the office, here's a picture of it, give me your credit card, okay? It's just very simple, very easy, I'm not trying to go and make this more complicated than that, okay? All right, and then in the webinar funnel, after it's live, we usually put up a replay page, it was a simple page, and click funnel is replay page, and we usually will give them some urgency and scarcity, like hey, the replay is gonna be up for two days, go check it out. And we'll have a couple emails saying, talking about urgency and scarcity, this page, we're pulling the replay down, in 24 hours, in 12 hours, in three hours it's gone, and then that pushes people through the cycle of urgency and scarcity, okay? That's basically how the webinar funnel works. And I said, that's how we built click funnels, it was interesting as, if you look at year one of click funnels, when the year ended, we had 10,000 members in click funnels, and what happened is we had about 2,500 people who bought click funnels from the webinar, who had a thousand dollar version back then, so 2,500 people bought from the webinar, and from that, remember on the thank you page, I told people to go get a free trial, for every person that bought the thousand dollar version of click funnels, what was the number, three people stuck on the trial, okay? So the end of year one, we had 2,500 people pay a thousand bucks, and we had 7,500 people who were on trial paying 97 dollars a month, okay? And that's how, that was the foundation of how we built and launched and grew click funnels, okay? So that's why webinar funnels are so powerful. Now there's a variation for those of you guys who, either have webinar presentation that's killing it, or you don't wanna do live webinars, which I can go on a rant on another day, you should do live webinar at least like, a hundred times before you automate it, but when you're ready, you can create an automated webinar funnel, which is very similar with some different intricacies, okay? So basically, the way an auto webinar funnel works, someone registers, and then the webinar actually happens in click funnels, it's a video presentation, of your webinar happens on click funnels, and if they're pushed in the order form, okay? It's very simple, so for example, this is our funnel scripts webinar, we did it live, Jim Edwards and I did it live initially, through a regular webinar funnel, then when it was done, we took that presentation that was recorded, it converted great, we put it here, so we made a webinar registration page, notice this one saying, hey, the webinar's starting today at 10.30 a.m., so you got eight minutes before it starts, so come register, they register it, and it takes the next page, oh, and then here's another variation, this is our secretfunnelstrategy.com, same thing, we got a webinar right here, they register for it, and they get a 48 hour pass, it says, you can register, you got 48 hours to watch this presentation, okay? And then boom, it from there takes them to the actual broadcast room, where they're, basically it's the webinar video embedded on the page, this is me and Jim doing our webinar on the page, this is me at the 10x growth con doing my presentation, videos embedded on the page, and they're both there. All right, so the third funnel type is a product launch funnel. Now, product launch funnel was made famous by my friend, Jeff Walker. And Jeff Walker, he actually, when we launched the dot-com secret book, initially he won the affiliate contest, and I gave away a Ferrari that I'd won in another affiliate contest, and he won it. So, Jeff is a really, really smart guy, and he's pioneered this, it's been used by so many people. It's not my personal favorite funnel type, just because I love webinars so well, but people who like product launches, they kill it, and I've done a bunch of them as well. I'll kinda show you guys the process, because it is a really powerful funnel type. So number one, here's the funnel structure. This is the framework of what a product launch funnel looks like, okay? The very first page is a free workshop where you get someone's email address, okay? The lead magnet is this workshop, okay? They opt in, and then there's a video, number one, there's a video, and people leave comments down below, and then a couple days later, they get video two with comment, and people can comment down below. A couple days later, video three, they comment down below, and then a couple days later, they get a video four, where they make an offer, and then it takes them to an order form, and then from there they buy the product, okay? So that's what the product launch funnel looks like. Jeff Walker calls this a sideways sales letter, which is kinda interesting. So he says basically, typically most people with long form sales letter selling it, he's like, all I'm doing is I'm tipping this sales letter on the side, boom, and the video number one is me teaching the very first part of my sales letter. Video two is the second part of the sales letter. Video three would be the third part, and video four is the fourth part, okay? So he's extending this consumption of this training slash selling over a period of time, okay? It might be each day they get a new video, somebody says every three days, every five days they get a new video, but that's kinda how it's structured and how it's set up, okay? All right, so let me show you how this works. So page number one is your lead manual, giving away free workshop, okay? So, hey, Optin here, I'm gonna give you free workshop about how to build your funnels quickly, wherever it might be, right? So that's video number one. They put their name and email address in, and then boom, now it unlocks the lead manual, which is this workshop, okay? So video number one, boom, they get the first video, which is the first part of the workshop, and they get video number two a couple days later, video number three a couple days later, and that's kinda works. The semi-is might be wondering, well Russell, what do I say in those videos, okay? You're never gonna guess, okay? Literally, you tell your stories, right? You tell your Piffin and Bridge stories about your thing, okay? What's interesting is when I do mine, I use the same perfect webinar framework, okay? Video number one is my origin story and my story about the vehicle. That's video number one. Video number two is talking about the internal beliefs. Video number three, that's going into the external beliefs, and number four is doing the stack and the close, okay? So again, if you've studied the perfect webinar or read the expert secrets book, you know this framework already, it's just taking those stories in that order, and now video number one is your origin and vehicle. Video two is internal beliefs, video three is external beliefs, and video four is your stack and your close, okay? It's again, he calls the sideways sales letters, the same thing, I'm taking this structure sales presentation, this proven work over and over and over again, I'm just tipping it outside, I'm getting them this first chunk first, second chunk later, third chunk later, fourth chunk later, okay? And if I do this presentation right, I do the video one, two, three, four correctly, then my stories will trump theirs, and their big dollars are gonna fall, and they're gonna go to the order form, and they're gonna buy my product, okay? And that's the product launch funnel, okay? Do you guys see how, if you look at any of the funnels here, the presentation funnels, they're not, there's not something brand new you gotta learn or discover, right? It's taking, here's my presentation, there's different ways I can show that. Number one is I show it just as a video sales letter, right? Or number two, I do it as a webinar, so I'm a registrar, and I do that presentation live in a live webinar, right? Or number two and a half is I take my live one, and I replicate the process, and I'm building up that auto webinar structure, but I'm building it out in an automated webinar, right? Or number three is I'm taking that video, that presentation, I'm chopping up into video one, two, three, and four, okay? But it's the same presentation, it's just three different ways to deliver that presentation, okay? So that's what's so core to understand. This level of the value ladder, we're selling something from 500 to a couple thousand dollars, you sell it through a presentation, the presentation is the same, okay? If you've been geeking out with me at all over the last couple of years, talking about perfect webinar, expert speakers, all this stuff, like that presentation has not changed or shifted at all, and what I own, all that's done is you're taking that presentation, you're delivering it through a couple different ways, okay? And each of them, there's pros and cons of every single one of them, but that shows you the frameworks of how they work. Throughout the year, most of us do not see them, often questioning their existence if it weren't for their countless retargeting acts. They wake in the early morning hours and are usually found in a crowded gym, mentoring the curious young hacklings, rejoicing in the sun by the watering hole for a brief moment. And of course, recording more acts because the backdrop is just stunning. They congregate in what's known to scientists as a joint venture. The outfit of choice being matching t-shirts and undergarments, signifying the bond that ties this dispersed breed together. This is amplified by a greeting ritual of an endless myriad of high fives, hugs, acro yoga, and handshakes, from the field to the bush. In the early hours, they descend upon one single enclosure. As the doors swing open, the hackers spring into motion, flocking 30 miles per hour in a race toward seat supremacy. Only the most spirited will lay claim to a coveted view. From there, they revel at the arrival of the alphas who impart their wisdom from prior hunts. During this time, their feeding ritual is complex. Many subspecies survive on a diet of coffee and water, which often unintentionally creates a conflict of having to choose between absorbing knowledge and going to the bathroom. Welcome back to session number six of the funnelology master class, the new art and science of funnel building. I hope you've been enjoying all these sessions so far. We've covered a lot of stuff. And hopefully it's helping us really understand these frameworks. I think a lot of times people think this marketing stuff is super complicated or hard or frustrating. And it's like, well, in reality, it's simple frameworks. And just understanding like, oh, here's the framework. It's how they fit together. Here's what I'm going to pick. And here's what I'm going to focus on. And then inside of there is where you have a chance to kind of weave your products, your services, your art into those frameworks. And so now we'll be walking you guys through the last framework here inside of the value ladder. So for you guys who are just tuning in now for the first time, session and wall, we talked a lot about the secret formula and Hook's story offer. Number two, we talked about the value ladder. And then three, four, five, and now six, we're talking about all the funnels that each step in the value ladder. And now we're moving up to the last, the back end, which are our phone funnels. And phone funnels are fun because if you're looking at how in the world do I sell a $10,000 product? People tell me all the time, like, well, Russell, I sell a $10,000 thing or $100,000 consulting or I'm selling big, huge, whatever projects, right? They're too expensive. I guess it's not going to work through a funnel. I'm like, no, you understand, like these work perfectly through a funnel. Every type of thing you're trying to sell will work in a funnel. You just have to be strategic and think about how is this going to work for me? Okay, by, for some reason, our default in our brain as humans is to have something come to us and like, oh, that's not going to work for me. And instead of stopping and saying, well, how could this work for me? And by shifting your question, it shifts everything inside your mind. Literally like any company can come to me and within two or three minutes, they're like, oh, just do a phone funnel, but shift it like this and make it work here. Or do the, like it's really simple when you start understanding the frameworks. And so if any of you guys are still stuck at this point, you're like, I don't know how this is going to work for me. And you're thinking like, how can this work? Like how, like how would I do this funnel? Is there a way I can make this funnel work for me? Like what would I have to tweak? What changes I have to make? How do I apply my art to this structure to make it so it's actually going to work for me? And that's the magic. That's why I love funnels. They're so much fun because there's like the art and the science. They both kind of tie together. And when you do it together, it's fun and you can make a ton of money obviously with it. So here's kind of recap. We started at lead funnels, the bottom value ladder, then session, now session three, session four, we did unboxing funnels, session five, we did presentation funnels. Now we are at the top of the value ladder inside of our phone funnels. That's what we're going to be hanging out today inside of phone funnels. And so honestly, inside of phone funnels, only one funnel that we're really going to show. There's a couple of variations of it, so you'll be able to learn some different ways to do it. But it really is just one pretty simple funnel. Okay. And so inside of phone funnels, what we're going to talk about today is a couple things. Number one, we're going to talk about a concept called takeaway selling. This is one of the keys to understand. I'm going to show you guys the case study phone funnel. I'm going to show you guys, there's two ways out. One is the case study or number two is the success story. So takeaway selling, the best way to explain this is typically when you're selling something, what are you doing? You were like chasing after someone, hey, do you want to buy this thing, do you want to buy this thing, do you want to buy this thing, you're going after them. Takeaway selling is where you make some amazing offer and you walk away. It's probably not for you if you think you want to let me know, but you're kind of pushing away. Okay. And I think a lot of times selling is a bad rep because it's the used car salesman, the cliche of like going out there and like trying to pitch you and trying to push you really, really hard. That way is not the best way to sell. And takeaway selling is a little bit different when you understand psychology, but it makes it so much more powerful, gives you a better positioning and it's just, it's better all around. So to kind of put this in perspective, this is, if you rewind probably eight or nine years ago now, I'd built up my very first sales company. We had a sales floor. I had 60 full-time sales people. This is a picture from that sales floor. And all these people, what would happen, somebody would buy something online and then we'd call them on the phone, like, oh, you bought this free DVD, you bought this free book, you bought this CD, whatever. Hey, do you want to buy coaching? And we started selling coaching through this process. We were sales people. We were going after our potential customers and selling them. Okay. And took a 60 full-time people to be able to call and generate revenue. Okay. Now fast forward a couple of years after we kind of started this whole process to build up and turns out that managing 60 full-time sales people is the nightmare. It's horrible. It's miserable. Don't ever do it. At least not me. And this eventually shut the whole thing down. And I was like, I will never do that again. That was really painful. But the nice thing about it was we were selling five, 10, 15, 25,000-dollar things on the phone. So I saw the value and the power of like, man, if I get something on the phone, I can sell something way more expensive. Okay. Because it's more intimate, I can make a sale one-on-one. I can figure out their exact concerns. I can adjust those concerns. So it was really powerful. But the problem is like, I had to manage 60 full-time people to do it. And so fast forward a couple of years after, I took like a two or three-year break. We didn't sell anything on the phones. And then we had this idea. So what if we tried to figure out a way to build a funnel that would replace all these 60 full-time sales people? What if there's a way to do that? And so we built a sales funnel. And basically what it did is it created a process where instead of us calling and like chasing people to sell them something, it created amazing leads, application leads. They told us all information about their name, their phone number, how much money they make, where they live, what their goals are like. And these leads came to us. It wasn't just like a name, email lead, but a whole full lead where I knew everything about them. I knew their desires, their pain points, and everything. And they were pretty sold on why they should work with me. So we started creating these application leads. And then instead of having 60 sales guys, I had just two. And what's crazy is after about a year of us testing and refining and perfecting this process, we were able to get to make as much money with two sales people and the sales funnel that we did with 60 full-time sales people before. It was really, really amazing. And so it all comes down to this process of takeaway selling. So let me explain takeaway selling small. Number one is that when you're doing takeaway selling, you are not selling them. They are selling you. Okay? That's the shift. All right. So the question then you guys start asking yourself is what is the result that your dream customer wants? Right? At the top of the value ladder, right? They've come through here. We talked about this day number one, right? You got the bottom value ladder coming up here. Now you're at the top right here. And this is the big result. Like what is the big result that they want? Right? So for me, if you come into my world, the big result you want is you want to be able to have a funnel that's going to grow your company, right? For you, it might be I want to lose weight. I want to be able to invest in finance. It's better. Like whatever it is, what's the big result that somebody's going to get if they start working with you? So you have to figure out what is that big result? And then the next question you're going to ask yourself is what is the case study of how you've already achieved that for yourself or for others? Okay? My guess is if you're selling this, hopefully you've already done this thing yourself, right? I've already made a million dollars in a funnel. I've already lost a bunch of money. I've already achieved this thing. I'm going to show you through my value ladder how to get this result, right? And so the first case is how have you done it for yourself? But the second one's even better. It's how have you done it for others? Okay? That's the key to how to make this funnel work. Okay? And so the first process I'm going to show you is what we call a case study funnel. So a case study funnel works. It's very simple. It's one of our high ticket funnels. The very first page right here is I'm going to share a case study about how I already got this result or how somebody I worked with got this result because of working with me. Okay? So I have the case study right here. If you go through the case study, they watch it. At the end of the case, they're like, man, I want the same result that he got or the same result that person that they talked about got. Okay? So then they apply a button, they put the email address, and then the second page is taken over here where they have a long form application. Here's where they're going to apply to actually work with you to help them get this exact same result that you got for yourself and for others. And the third page is the homework page. Okay? So we'll walk you through it. So this is an example of an old funnel we had. Right here at the very top, there's a video. And this was if you read it here, it says a free case study of how this one girl made $89,230 to a tiny list of people in less than three days. Okay? So she was one of our clients. And so I made a video that showed her process. It's like, hey, she was this person, she had a tiny list, didn't think it was going to be possible. We set this funnel, we set this process. This is what happened. They number one, we did this. They number two, we did this. They number three, we did this. And then all of a sudden, the time was money she made from that tiny list in three days. If you want to learn how to do this, you want me to help you with this, go apply down below. That was it. Share a case study, tell them to apply if they want our help doing the same thing inside of their business, inside of their own life, whatever it is you're selling. Okay? So that's page number one. Okay? Again, they're going to watch the video of the case study, and they're going to apply if they want my help or your help getting that same result. And then we get their email address so we can then follow up them throughout the process. Okay? All right, so that's page number one. Now we're going to move on to the application page. The application page now is where they fill out a bunch of questions about why we should work with them. Okay? I've gotten myself this result. I've got other people like, let me know why you think I should work with you to help you get that result as well. Because I don't want to work with everybody, I want to work with the right people. Why are you the right person to work with me to get that result? Okay? And they're going to go and then sell themselves. They're going to sell you on why they should actually work with you. That's what the application is about. Okay? So the application, a couple of things, is number one, you want to explain what it looks like to work with you. Okay? So if I do happen to pick you, this is going to look like when you call everyone's while it's going to be a meeting, it's going to be events, whatever it is, that you're high in packages, explain what it's going to look like to work with you. We're going to have a full application with them to apply. And inside the application, there's a lot of different questions you can ask. When we first started building this, I started looking at like every kind of application out there. And I found a couple of things. The application questions I ask, I ask some questions like, what type of business do you own? Am I having something on a revenue qualifier? So like, revenue qualifier, I might ask something like, you know, how much money a month do you spend on ads right now? Or how much money do you have to invest in your business? Okay? If you're in a different type, if you're not in a business type situation, maybe you're in fitness or finance, revenue qualifier might be like, how much money do you spend eating out per month? Like what's your budget for healthy food every single month? So you find out like, are they making enough money to actually afford what I'm doing? It's something like, I can't buy healthy food, we can't afford it. Then you have a revenue qualifier knowing like, this person is probably going to be a big fit for my $30,000 weight loss program, right? Or if they're like, hey, we don't spend any money on Facebook ads because we can't pay the bills, they're not going to be investing in my hiring program. I'm going to tell them to go buy my book instead or something that's that that is that they can actually afford at a time. Okay? So I have a revenue qualifier. I like asking questions to find out what success looks like for them. Can we have big problems you have when you're trying to sell some really expensive things? Is it come in and you're like, okay, this is our program is going to help you build a funnel. But in their mind, like, if I sign up for this program, it means I'm going to have a billion dollar funnel. I'm going to have, you know, a hundred thousand who leads a day and have like all like they have their vision of successes. And if it's different than what your vision is, what you're actually fulfilling on and there's a discrepancy, no matter how good you are at fulfilling on this thing that you're going to do, if their vision is something over here, you're never going to be successful together. Okay? So like asking questions like, what does success look like for you? If I decide to work with you and try to help you get this result as well, what does success look like for you? Okay? A lot of times I'll have people who seem like traditional, amazing business people and they'll apply for a coaching program with us and like my, you know, right now I'm making, you know, a hundred fifty thousand dollars a year, which is pretty good. But you know, I want to make sure my take home is no less than a million dollars a year. And then like, that's, you know, within the next 12 months. I'm like, you know what, the likelihood of you getting a million dollar a year take home after all your expenses and everything is probably not very high, especially in the first year. Like, I don't think I can fulfill on that. So I might say like, I'm not going to work with you because your goals are way out of skew. I had one guy, this is kind of, I mean, a couple of them in mind. He was like, he's doing good. He had like a good corporate business and he's, he wanted to make sure that, that after his advertising costs, all his hard costs and everything, his take home is no less than three to five million dollars a year. And I'm like, like to do that, that's awesome. But you got to build a business like 70, 80, a hundred million dollars. We can give you that eventually, but not in the next 12 months. Like that's like, you have to be realistic. So I want to make sure that I understand what success is like with them so I can see, am I going to be able to fulfill that or not? And if I can't fulfill it, I do not want them as a client. Okay. So it gives me an idea really quickly to find out what, if they're a good fit for me. One of the easiest ways to find this out, there's a book called The Question by Dan Sullivan and the book's amazing. The gist is this is the question. You ask them this question. In fact, if you look at any of my coaching applications, they always ask this. It says, if we're having this discussion three years from today and you're looking back over those last three years, what would have happened into your life both personally and professionally for you to feel happy with the progress? Okay. They come back and say, look, I just want to have more impact on the world right now. I'm doing well. I'd love to have a company that's at least a million bucks a year. Hopefully my take home is 30% of that or whatever it is. I'm hoping to be able to blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, when I'm bigger impact on this and we're talking to my family, I'm like, boom, I can help you. Those are things that I can help you do very, very easily. So I focus on that. If it's a good fit, that might cool. We're going to be working together. If they're way outside of what's reality, then I'm like, this is probably not going to be a good relationship. Okay. And then also usually when I'm selling coaching, there's some kind of group dynamic. I want to make sure that they're not just takers, but also givers. So I ask like, why would you be a good fit for this group? You're going to be working with a lot of other people just like you. Like, why would you, like, let me know why you're a good fit. And then they sell you on like, why would you be a good fit because of this and this and this. It helps to make sure that they're a team player, that they're a giver, not just a taker. Okay. And you had to be a good fit for the group. How would you contribute things like that? So those are the types of questions I ask in my application. Okay. So again, page number one, case study, page number two, application. The page number three is the homework. Okay. Now a couple of things on the homework. One is I give them homework that helps to build the connection with me. So I might say, Hey, we go watch this video to tell you the story. So a lot of times I have a video to tell about why I got in this business. One of the videos I usually use on my thank you pages is me telling my story about my wife and our kids and how we got, how we couldn't have babies and we, we got fertility and we ended up having twins and how that doctor that changed my life because of, because of what she was doing. And I tell this whole story and it's emotional. I cry like three or four times in the movie and the video is kind of embarrassing, but it builds connection with people. Right. So I give homework assignments to go watch trainings and watch things I've done so they can have better connection with me. Okay. That's number one. And number two is I try to get them to initiate an inbound call. Obviously the application comes in, I'm going to outbound call them, but we found, back when I had the call center with 60 sales people, we found that if I was calling them on the phone back then, the metrics are different today, but we would average about $150 per lead. If I had them to call me, we're averaging about $450 per lead. Okay. So whatever the math is, like three times more money by getting them to initiate the call. So even though I'm going to call every application that comes in, I'm going to tell them on this page like, hey, if you want to jump to the front of the line, if you're really excited and you're going to get started today, like call this number does not mean you're guaranteed a spot at all, but it means you're going to jump to the front of the line and you can talk to somebody and see if you're going to fit right now. Okay. And it gets them to initiate an inbound call. And so I try to get them to initiate an inbound call as quick as we can. And so that's kind of what case study phone funnel looks like. Case study, application, homework, where I build connection and get them to initiate a phone call. Okay. And that's it. Very, very simple. Case study phone call. Instead of doing a case study of like, let me tell you this case study of how I got myself this result, some else's result. The next variation is just what we call a success story. Okay. The funnel is very, very simple, similar, but instead of telling a full case study, I'm having someone tell a success story. Okay. So some of you guys have seen this funnel before. This is a video of Liz Benny from page number one. But notice like this is just me changing the art. The funnel structure is the same, right? The frameworks are all the same. Just here instead of a case study, I have Liz Benny telling a story and they apply. Then page number two, where I have a video telling my story and then they fill out a longer form application. And from there, there's a picture, a video of me telling my story and then trying to get them to create an inbound call. Okay. So page number one is success story. So this is Liz Benny. Now, I'm going to actually show you guys this video. One thing about when I show you this, I want you to understand, notice that it wasn't like, you know, I worked with Russ when I got rich, quick, you know, and in-complain, in-complain, it's not me telling much in-complain, it's things like that. Okay. As your company grows, you're going to get a lot of trouble for that. This is her telling her actual success story. Okay. And if you watch this video, like it's an amazing video. It's made me millions and millions of dollars. But what's interesting is it's not her just talking about how she got rich. It's her talking about how hard it was and how work can be helped during a changed life. But man, it was, it was, it was this painful process. And what's nice about this was someone goes to watch this video, when they come into coaching later, they're not like, oh, I thought this was going to be super easy. I was going to get rich quick. You know, it's like, no, I watched the video. Liz framed this whole coaching program. It's going to be works, going to be painful, but man, it's going to be worth it. Right. And it's going to be different, a better quality lead coming through. So let me, we'll show you guys this video. So you can see just an example of a case study video, excuse me, a success story video that we used to fill up a whole coaching program. So I want to tell you something. But you know what the eventuality is that sometimes in life, it doesn't always work out the exact way that you want it to work out. And that's certainly where it got to for me. So my 2014 was one of the hardest years I've ever had in my entire life. Like there was cancer in the family. People were in hospital. I thought I was going to, um, I, at one stage, I, um, you know, I thought I was going to lose my partner. Like it just wasn't a pleasant scenario at all. And by losing my partner, I mean like hospitalization, like really not like from really bad health issues. And you know, there were just times when I promised you I wanted to give up. And sometimes I actually, when I say that, I don't just mean give up in business. Like I, I, for me, when I, like when I hired Russell, it really was like do like sounds really dramatic. But like my life's mission, it's so, it's so big. Like I really, really, really wanted to connect with someone who could get me to the next level, who could help me really get from that really stranded, grounded place, you know, sitting at my office every single day to a place where I'm, you know, helping like people all around the world. And that's why I chose Russell. Um, but man, it was hard. It was really, really, really hard at times to have to deal with those things that come up in your personal life all the time. Like the man has changed my life forever. Like once, you know, once year, like my mind has been blown completely. And, and there's no way back. Like I, I know that anything is possible now. So you just see how powerful that is, the success story of someone who's gone through your process and you share that. Um, and again, it's not tons of income claims and things like that or, you know, weight loss, cladding, those things, just them telling their, their story and their journey and the right people are going to hear that and they're going to come and they're going to, they're going to come into your funnels. Okay. And so that's what the success story funnel looks like. I want you to start thinking about for any kind of business, like whenever people come to me like, well, my business is different because we don't sell things online or we don't whatever. You get somebody to watch a case study of something you've done for yourself or for somebody else or short, a success story where they have this amazing frame and they give me the email address and they go to the next page and they're telling you like why you should work and they're selling themselves and you're like, you should pick me because of this and because of this and this is what success looks like. And this is why do you get to contribute to the group and they're selling themselves on why you should pick them. Okay. Then you get the lead. Now imagine any kind of business that's got any kind of phone sales team, right. I know they're outbound calling leads. They're calling cold calling people that are getting, you know, buying leads offline or whatever it is, like calling that kind of lead versus calling this kind of lead where somebody is amazing and they're prepared and they're ready to work with you and they're telling you why you should pick them. Like it shifts everything. We went from us outbound calling leads averaging $150 DPL, dollar per lead to we shifted to this and now we average over $1,500 per application, 10 times as much money by taking them through this process. Okay. Again, I went from 60 sales guys to two, making the exact same amount of revenue. Okay. Do you see the power behind that? Like that's what this process does. It generates and creates the most amazing dream lead you can dream of. Okay. So even if you're not selling coaching, it doesn't matter if you're selling anything where you're calling leads on the phone. If you've taken them through this process, you're washing the people through this process to prepare them, to indoctrinate them, to warm them up and get them selling you on why you should allow them to buy your product, your service.
