Like why don't you sell coaching? Like it doesn't matter if you sell coaching. Okay. If you're calling people on the phone and trying to sell anything, man, take them through this process initially and that the lead becomes worth 10 times as much money to you or more. Okay. And the other amazing thing is instead of having 60 salespeople, I'm only getting the cream of the crop. The best people are coming through and the best leads come through. Okay. I'm not wasting all this energy with 60 people calling everybody to try to find out who's actually ready. The funnel is all the work, the sifting, the sorting, the preparation, the pre-work, the pre-swage, all those things happen and we're just giving this amazing piece of gold at the end. Okay. All right. So now we have this gold and the question is like, what do we say on the phone? Okay. Now again, this is outside of the context of this training. We have a product called High-Ticket Secrets that goes deep into this. We also inside the dot-com Secrets book, I go deep into both of the different phone scripts. I'm going to kind of go through them really quickly here. There's two scripts. Number one is what we call the four question clothes. Okay. The four question clothes works if you don't have a salesperson. If it's just you, okay, or maybe you have one salesperson. Okay. So the application comes in and there's these four questions that we go through. Okay. Again, question number one, notice is very similar to the application. Question number one is all about, hey, if we were to have this conversation in three years from now, we're looking back last year, what would have happened both professionally and personally and professionally for you to feel like this was successful? Okay. And they come back and they start dreaming about the future and visualizing what's going to be possible. Okay. And if they're asking, okay, well, what's keeping you back right now? Like, why are you hitting that? Like, well, I'm not doing it because I'm stuck here. I'm stuck here. They're talking about the things that's keeping them from the results that they want. And you say, okay, based on that, like, what are the resources you have that you haven't tapped into yet? Like, what are you, like, like, tell me about what you have. And they're like, well, you know, I have an email list. We don't really email. I've got this. I've got this. They're telling you all the things that they have through the untapped resources so you know exactly how to work with them and say, cool. And then the next question is like, well, do you think based on what you know of me so far, do you think that I can help you get that goal? Right? Like, I see where you're trying to go. I see where you're stuck. I know resources you have. I hope you get there. Would you like my help with that? Typically, they say yes and then they say, cool. Well, let's see you signed up. Okay? And again, the book goes into more detail about this. I'm not a phone sales person, so my team is the ones who develop these questions and they go deep into it. So if you read the dot com secrets book, it'll go deeper. But this is the four question close. Okay? So if you've got one sales person, this is the script we use. Now the other thing we do is we, for most of our clients, we try to set up both a setter and a closer. Okay? If you do that, the script's a little bit different. And I'm not going to go deep into the script because it's, again, it'll take me a while to go and do it and I don't know as well as the sales people. But again, in the dot com secrets book, it's all in there. But basically there's two scripts. Number one is the setter. The setter is the first person to actually get on the phone and the setter's job is to qualify and see if they're actually a good fit for your program. So they go through these first eight parts. They have the intro, they ask questions, they go through the blasts, the posture, the probe, the finding credit, the goals and the commitments, find out if they are a good fit. Okay? This way that person is going through finding out if they're a good fit. If they are a good fit, then the person hands them off to the closer and the closer goes to the next set of questions to figure out how to get them in the program, make sure that everything's going to work out good. Okay? So it's a two person setter, a closer script. This is the best script when you're not doing any of the selling. Okay? Told you before that I'm scared to death of getting on the phone. I'm very introverted. I didn't want to do these kind of things. And so when we got started, I had setter and a closer and it gave the ability to position a posture me. Okay? With the four questions script back here, a lot of times it's the guru. It's you on the phone doing the sell. That's why we do the four question close. Okay? So at the same time, there's always a decrease in positioning when you're the one doing the call. If you're also the one who's doing the coaching or whatever thing at the end, you're like, you have time to do a phone call. Like it kind of depositioned you. But a lot of times this is where people start. And then as they grow a little bit better and they step away from the sales role, they used to shift to this where the setter and the closer are all about edifying you and posturing and positioning you. Okay? The way the script works, it increases your intrinsic value by the way that the script works. And when someone's done and they go through this process, then they come to you. So for me, when we started this new sales process, again, I didn't want to be on the phones. I'm scared to have to be on the phone. So we set up the setter and closer and did tens of millions of dollars in sales. So I'm going to watch this video. They'd apply. The setter would get on the phone. So they're going to fit. It's so pass them off to the closer, closer, closer, sell. And then they'd have a chance to come work with me. And I was able to help them at that level. Okay? So that's kind of the process. But again, these funnels are very simple. Okay? Either case study of what you've done or hyping up for others or success story. They watch that. They give the email address to work with you. Set number two is they will go through a full long form application where they're selling you on why you should work with them. Step three, you give them homework to build connection and you try to create an inbound call. And then during that inbound call or when you outbound call them, then you either, if you're one person sales team, you go through the four question clothes. If you're two person sales team, then you go through the setter, closer script. And that's it. That's the magic how tag ticker funnels work. The cool thing about high ticket funnels like this is it gives you the ability to sell things at all sorts of levels. Okay, we first started doing this back, man, a decade ago. We were selling things for $5,000. But I thought it was like, that's so much money. No one ever buy anything besides five. Later on, after we shut down the big call center, when we opened the small two person call center, initially we started selling things at $8,000. And it blew my mind that the scripts worked. So you get people to buy eight. And we raised the prices to 10, from 10 to 12, and then 12 to 15, 15 to 25, and 20 to five to 50 and beyond. And what we found is these scripts work at every single price point. We've got people now who are selling $100,000 offers with a million dollar offers within a year, and it works all throughout. So if you are trying to sell high ticket stuff, it is the key. It is the goal. It is the secret. Okay? And so that's kind of the end of this funnrology training. To kind of recap this and wrap it up with you guys, everything that we've talked about, I want you guys just to remember that the funnels aren't some magic. They're frameworks. The frameworks that work there, it's just like a framework for a house. If you want to build that house, there's a framework. And then you build the walls and stuff, then you go and you put in your own wallpaper, your own curtains, and all those kind of things. If you look at these sessions we talked about, session number one was all about, okay, who was your dream customer? What does that person look like? Number two is where are they congregating? How do we, how do we, two-eyed fish, I guess all fish have two eyes. Where are they congregating at? Number three is what is the bait you're throwing in to capture that person? And then inside of the bait, we have the framework of hook story offer, right? And then from there, it's like after we found that person now, how do we send them up and how do we give them a certain result, right? And then here we said, okay, now, that was session number one. Then from there it's okay, based on this result, now how do we get the person up there? Now we have this value ladder, right? So it's like, okay, here's the value ladder and here's how we're going to move somebody through this tier of things so we can give them this big result, right? And then from there it's okay, well, if we move somebody through this value ladder, well, what are the funnels of this step, right? Here we have our lead funnels. They help generate a lead. Now, if they go through the lead funnel, then they naturally send up the next funnel, which are unboxing funnels, right? We have these unboxing funnels of the next step. After they go through the unboxing funnels, then it sends them up to our presentation funnels, right? Here's our TV, there's our presentation funnels. We give a presentation from there, we send up to our phone funnels, okay? But everything in this entire process, if you look at it, is all about just this framework right here. Like who are your dream customers? Who do you actually want to work with? Who are the people that you are passionate about? Like if you can sit there all day and figure out what else can you do to help them, what else can you do to help them? Like you can figure out those people, like, and you figure them out, that's the first step. And then it's like, okay, now I've got to find those people. Let me find them, like, oh my gosh, here they are. Oh, they're over here. Now I found them. Here's my people. These people want to serve people, I want to help people want to work with. Now let me throw my bait out there to grab them, get their tension. Now I've got their tension. Now let me take them through this process where I can help them get a result. I can change their life. And that's what business is all about. Business should not be a transactional thing. So many people teach and they show and they talk about, especially in college and in like business classes, it's all about transactional. Here's like, here I got money in and I'm turning it back and this is transactional thing. It's not transactional, it's transformational. As soon as you shift your business from, I'm going to try to transact and make money. So I'm going to try to transform the lives of the dream customers that I've been called to serve. That's when everything will shift for you. That's the magic. That's what you've got to understand. And then all this funnel thing is no longer complicated. It's like, okay, I know who they are, I know where they're at. I'm grabbing them, telling them hooks are after pull them in here and here's the funnel framework that's going to send them up so I can get them a certain result. And at this level, these are the frameworks that are possible. I'm going to pick that one and we might add into it. And then from here, I'm going to pick this one and we might add into there. Pick this one, we're going to go through the process and it becomes simple and very, very easy. It's just frameworks. It's just structures. Just things right out there, we're going to grab it and then from there, put our messaging, put our stories into it. The last big caveat I want to say, I went kind of on a rant on this during the funnel alliging training a couple of days ago in session number two was a lot of times we were looking at this and say, okay, we want to get our dream customer up here. To do that, I need to build the whole entire value ladder out before I get started. And I want to just restate. That's not the goal. The goal of me showing you these 10 funnels is for you to look at all of these. We had three here, we had funnel one, two and three here, we had funnel one, two and three here, one, two and three here and funnel one here. So you have all these different funnels. My goal is for you to see a landscape of like, okay, here's what's possible. Maybe in the next three years, five years, 10 years, I'm going to have a full value ladder looks like this, but today I just need one. What's the one funnel I'm going to focus on right now? And so hopefully you've identified that. You figured out like this is the one that makes the most sense to me, but maybe you haven't. Maybe you're still kind of struggling. So if you've got to think back, for my type of business, what makes the most sense? If I'm like an author, speaker, coach, consultant, for almost all of you guys, I'm going to say start right here with like a webinar funnel. That's going to be the fastest path, the cash for you. I'd focus all my energy on this webinar funnel and do nothing else until that funnel's made at least a million dollars. We got into two column of club after two column of club, then you go back and you can build a high end, back end funnel to get these people to send up to you. Or you can build a down cell and do a book funnel, get somebody in a book funnel to get more people into your webinar, things like that. Okay, but that's where I'd start and that's where I'd focus and I would do only that one funnel to get at least a million dollars in sales. Okay, if you're an e-commerce person, I'm not going to do a webinar. I'm going to come back here to the unboxing funnel and I'm going to say, look, I'm going to do a cart funnel. I'm going to e-commerce. Cart funnels make the most sense for me. So I'm going to build a cart funnel and focus all my energy, all my efforts on this one funnel and I'm going to start focusing on it, building it, building it until we get to the do-comic club. Now after the do-comic club, maybe I can step down and say, okay, let me build a couple of lead funnels, get more leads in to get people into this funnel. And maybe that's the business, right? Okay, some of you guys are like, well, I'm a service-based business. What should I do? Like, okay, well, if you're a service-based business, let's say you're a chiropractor, or dentist, or in Vizalign, right? Maybe you just start here with a lead funnel. Maybe you're like, no, I don't want some of this email address. I want my staff to call them on the phone. Okay, well, let's do a phone funnel right here. If you're a service-based business, just because you're selling $25,000 coaching, if you're selling $2,000 teeth whitening, like, hey, take them through a phone funnel. Make sure that the funnel is all the hard work of screening and preparing and getting them sold on why they should work with you, right? And get these leads that are hot that are coming into your funnel into your practice every single day and then call them and bring them in and sell them your $2,000, your $5,000, your $10,000 services and packages of cosmetic dentistry, of chiropractic, of Vizalign, like, whatever the thing is of LASICS, of like, whatever your business is, like, generate the dream leads to come in to be able to close whenever you're trying to sell, right? Again, every business type, I would plug into a different thing. If I'm a restaurant, what am I going to do? I'm probably going to do a phone funnel for a restaurant, but maybe I'm going to do a lead funnel giveaway coupons, and let's get a bunch of people coming into our restaurant, right? And I'm going to a bunch, I'm building a list of people who every single Tuesday at four when sales are down and nobody's showing up, I can send an email to that list and I can blow up the company. Okay, we had a friend who worked at a local pizza place, and the pizza place, all he did is, in fact, he was ordering pizza and it was dead, he showed up and it was like dead in there. And the guy was making his pizza and he was like, what do you do? And he's like, I'm an internet marketer. And the guy's like, well, how, you know, I'm a pizza guy, how would that work for my business? He's like, well, how do you generate leads now? He's like, oh, I run yellow page ads and you have these different ways to get people to come in. He's like, well, how is business? He's like, well, on the weekends, it's crazy because everyone wants pizza, so we're always packed, but Monday through Thursday is dead in here. And so my friend said, well, what if we build you out of the lead funnel? Every single person that comes in here for pizza, have this iPad here and have them put in their name and email address in for a chance to win free pizza or something like that. Right? And he said, just do that for next month and just keep people filling this form. Okay, so we had people filling this form for an entire month and they're all generating leads, and the lead's coming in, the second lead's coming in, the third lead's coming in. And so about 30 days came back and said, well, what's happened? And the guy said, well, last month I got 1,500 people to join my email list. And he's like, cool. And the guy's like, what do I do with it now? Like, I don't even know. He said, well, you have them on a list now, you have the ability to email them. So he said, well, you do it every single time it's slow in your restaurant, email the list and tell them to come in for special. And so the guy started every single day at like 3.30, he'd email his list of 1,500 people saying, hey, just so you know, dinner's coming really soon, the kitchen's going to be hopping, it's going to be busy. If you want to grab a pizza on your way home from the office, just respond back to the email, let me know what you want, and we'll have the pizza ready for you. And so he sent an email every single day at 3 o'clock, within seconds the email coming out, his phone's ringing. Phone's ringing, he started making pizzas, and he started, like, whenever he wanted to, he was able to fill his practice with people. Do you see the power of that? Because he started generating leads, he had a list of people, and I was able to get people into his restaurant whenever he wanted, as opposed to just waiting like, well, I hope somebody shows up today, which is what most businesses do. So all these funnels have different techniques, different ways that you can use them. It's just coming back to your own business thing, like, okay, let me be strategic, like, for my business, like, what's the end result? I'm trying to sell this product, okay? Am I going to sell it through a presentation? Well, it's a low ticket product, so maybe not, I'm going to sell it through here. Or, you know, I don't really need to sell something, but I need leads coming. Cool, let's do something like this, let's see what leads it. But like, oh, I want higher quality leads. Cool, let's do this one here. Let's give you the highest quality leads, okay? And you start understanding this, you guys. This gives you the ability to become a funnel consultant for yourself, for other people, to understand, like, this is how funnels work. Beneath the gaze of the Nashville Sun, life at Funnel Hacking Live begins to take form. A sight not to be missed. Here we can see the migration of nearly 5,000 Funnel Hackers descend upon an unsuspecting hotel. For a brief period of four days, this habitat will dramatically change. The Funnel Hacker form is a rare species, but ever growing by the thousands each passing day. Throughout the year, most of us do not see them, often questioning their existence if it weren't for their countless retargeting acts. They wake in the early morning hours and are usually found in a crowded gym, mentoring the curious young hacklings, rejoicing in the sun by the watering hole for a brief moment and of course recording more ads because the backdrop is just stunning. They congregate in what's known to scientists as a joint venture. The outfit of choice being matching t-shirts and undergarments, signifying the bond that ties this dispersed breed together. This is amplified by a greeting ritual of an endless myriad of high fives, hugs, acro yoga and handshakes from the field to the bush. In the early hours they descend upon one single enclosure. As the doors swing open, the hackers spring into motion, flocking 30 miles per hour in a race towards seat supremacy. Only the most spirited will lay claim to a coveted view. From there, they revel at the arrival of the alphas who impart their wisdom from prior hunts. During this time, their feeding ritual is complex. Many subspecies survive on a diet of coffee and water, which often unintentionally creates a conflict of having to choose between absorbing knowledge and going to the bathroom. As the voyage comes to an end, they celebrate with a multitude of unpolished dance rituals and whatever this is, a pleasing sensation. They have replenished their minds and spirits and are fit to return to the wild. FHL is a unique anthropological phenomenon, fit for the most peculiar of entrepreneurs.
